title: Understanding Terminal Velocity
text: Terminal velocity is the constant speed that a freely falling object eventually reaches when the resistance of the medium prevents further acceleration. It occurs when the force of gravity is balanced by the drag force.

title: Formula for Terminal Velocity
text: The formula for terminal velocity \(v_t\) is given by: \(v_t=sqrt((2*m*g)/(C*rho*A))\), where \(m\) is mass, \(g\) is acceleration due to gravity, \(C\) is the drag coefficient, \(rho\) is the air density, and \(A\) is the surface area.

title: Calculate the Mass in Kilograms
text: Convert the mass of the squirrel from grams to kilograms. $$\(m=560g\)$$

title: Calculate the Surface Area in Square Meters
text: Convert the surface area from square centimeters to square meters. \(A=650cm**2\)
